channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tip stone concrete rubble dumped, 31st March
item.description:
This has been reported numerous times and yet remains after A YEAR! It is too heavy for the street sweeper to remove. It is TFL's responsibility to remove!! It's an constant eyesore.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westhorne Avenue, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Side of number 9, 31st March
item.description:
Lots of furniture been dumped washing machines so far wardrobes et cetera et cetera

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Moye Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Not resolved, 31st March
item.description:
Reported fly tipping Infront of star mews gate got email saying it was fixed but problem not resolved as stuff still there

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Windus Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Christmas Tree on Street, 31st March
item.description:
Item has been left at current location for over a week

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Buttesland Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped wardrobe and other stuff, 31st March
item.description:
Fly-tipping. Please put some signs up.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nevill Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed dumped vwhicle, 31st March
item.description:
The vehicle was clamped for no tax but they removed the clamp and left it hidden opposite no 14
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Wren Close, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars blocking driveway double yellow blips, 31st March
item.description:
Happens every evening.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant fly tipping at the entrance to development, 31st March
item.description:
People are constantly fly tipping at the entrance to 291 Hackney Road. This week there have been multiple drops. A lot of the contents this week have been disposable blue roll cloths (as you will see from the photos) which indicates it’s a business nearby.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hackney Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More dumping from businesses, 31st March
item.description:
I feel like I’m reporting the same issue again and again as each week the same amount of rubbish is left by the businesses along Manor Road. I see them getting palates of goods delivered every day and every day they are dumping them outside

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manor Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 31st March
item.description:
Car hasn’t been moved for 5-6 weeks, left outside garages

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fulthorp Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on the payment, 31st March
item.description:
The rubbish bags are just dumped and broken so food is spilling out

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120, Shenley Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More dumping from businesses, 31st March
item.description:
I reported this same issue last week and yet again the businesses along manor parade/Manor Road are dumping their rubbish in the unused telephone box and footpath. Why are these businesses not getting fined or made pay for commercial waste bins

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manor Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 8 royal college Street NW1 0TH, 31st March
item.description:
Re: Issues 9197281 and 9186461 The defect is located in the carriageway of Royal College Street (as shown in the attached map), not within a housing estate. It concerns a defective road surface / utility cover which is causing repeated and significant noise disturbance from passing vehicles. Please can this be reassessed and correctly logged with the Highways team rather than Housing Repairs. I would also appreciate clarification on responsibility. The issue has been marked as “not the council’s responsibility”, however it remains within the public highway and is causing ongoing disturbance. If this asset belongs to a third party (e.g. utilities), please confirm: The responsible organisation That the issue has been formally raised with them Any reference number associated with that escalation In addition, please provide contact details for Lumen Technologies, as these are not publicly available and I have been unable to locate them. For context, I have previously contacted Virgin Media for a similar issue, and they responded within 12 hours. This issue has now been raised multiple times and remains unresolved. I would appreciate a clear route to resolution and confirmation of next steps.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Royal College Street, St Pancras, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 5 feet at the front of Roots of panjab grocery store, 31st March
item.description:
So, individuals sit on that bench and drinking alocholo also smokes everyday mostly which affect our buisness and uncomfortable for customers. Sometimes they listens loud music and create noises so please remove this chair. Because we are here from last 4 months and its continously happening everyday in picardy street DA17 5QQ.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Picardy Street, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike dumped 126 Cheviot gardens, 31st March
item.description:
See bike number in photo

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheviot Gardens, Brent Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned motor home, 31st March
item.description:
This vehicle seems to have been dumped I removed black tape from The licence plates that was changing the 66 into 88 it has been parked in our car park for at least two weeks and taking up much needed space for the residents cars. Please arrange removal

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Culling Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicles obstructing pavement, 31st March
item.description:
There are numerous vehicles in driveway and across pavement, also vehicles permanently parked on road opposite. The amount of vehicles is multiplying encroaching onto the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 50, Cray Valley Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Significant graffiti on walls, 31st March
item.description:
There are a number of instances of graffiti on the walls adjacent to the pavement. Many in red, black, white, green, and other colours.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tilston Bright Square, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 1Milman pothole, 31st March
item.description:
1Milman near junction with Harvist Pothole requires filling Ta

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Milman Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lampsost no 8, 31st March
item.description:
Cover hanging off lampost no 8 one of the EV charging ones has been vandalised
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Crayford High Street, Crayford, Dartford
